plan_metrics.plan_content:
The plan is written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/linked-napping-book.md`. Here's a summary of what will be built:

## Custom Theme System — 8 Files

### New files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/contracts/src/theme.ts` | `ThemeId`, `ColorThemeSelection`, `ThemeColorTokens`, `ThemeDefinition` interfaces |
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | Color data for all 3 themes + `resolveThemeId`, `getTheme` helpers |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Radio-card UI with per-theme color swatches |

### Modified files
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/contracts/src/index.ts` | Add `export * from "./theme"` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `./theme` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Full rewrite — CSS custom property application, new storage key, localStorage migration from old `"t3code:theme"` key |
| `apps/web/src/index.css` | Add `--sidebar`, `--editor`, `--terminal`, `--status-bar` CSS vars + Tailwind `@theme inline` tokens |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />` |

### Key design decisions
- **Types in contracts, data in shared** — follows the existing package separation exactly
- **CSS custom properties via inline styles** — `document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--background', ...)` overrides stylesheet rules; no build config or new dependencies touched
- **`.dark` class preserved** — toggled per `theme.isDark` so all existing Tailwind `dark:` variants keep working
- **`"system"` option retained** — maps to t3-light/t3-dark based on `prefers-color-scheme`
- **localStorage migration** — old `"t3code:theme"` key is transparently migrated to `"t3code:color-theme"` on first load

### The three themes
- **T3 Light** — matches existing CSS light mode defaults exactly (zero visual change for existing light-mode users)
- **T3 Dark** — matches existing CSS dark mode defaults exactly (zero visual change for existing dark-mode users)
- **Midnight** — deep navy/blue palette (`#080c14` bg, `#e2e8f0` text, bright blue primary `oklch(0.65 0.22 240)`)
